description_long: This is an irregularly shaped, roughly triangular stone with a pointed lower end and a broader, slightly concave upper edge. The surface is rugged and uneven, featuring a mix of light gray, off-white, and darker gray tones, with some areas appearing more weathered than others. One side displays a hand-written identification number, suggesting it may have been cataloged or collected for study. The stone's edges are sharp and angular, with visible flake scars and natural fractures, indicating it may have been chipped or broken from a larger piece. The texture is coarse and matte, with no visible polish or artificial treatment. Its size and form suggest it could serve as a natural obstacle or decorative element in an urban landscape.
description: A rugged, triangular stone with sharp edges, coarse texture, and a mix of gray and off-white tones, featuring a hand-written number on one side.
description_view_0: The front view shows the broadest face of the stone, highlighting its triangular shape, rough surface, and a mix of gray and white coloration.
description_view_1: The side view reveals the stone's thin profile and jagged, uneven edges, emphasizing its sharpness and flaked structure.
description_view_2: The back view displays a lighter, smoother surface with a visible hand-written number, and less pronounced textural variation.
description_view_3: The opposite side view again shows the stone's thinness and angularity, with a slightly darker coloration compared to the other side.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or scattered among other stones
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling and veining with natural flake scars; no artificial carvings or regular patterns.
attribute_weathering: Rough and unpolished with visible chipping and minor surface erosion, indicating moderate natural weathering.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with gravel or soil, may contrast with paved surfaces but integrates well with other natural elements.
